DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL DO(ES) NOT STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING HEARD BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. PLAINTIFF SARA CHEUNG'S MOTION TO QUASH DEFENDANTS SUBPOENA TO PLAINTIFFS CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS, COMMUNITY PSYCHIATRY. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 20, 2018 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302, LAW AND MOTION DEPARTMENT TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE HAROLD KAHN. JUDGE PRO TEM TO PREPARE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: JENNIFER MOSER ROUX, CSR #11033, 707-477-9599, REPORTED.(D302/JPT/HK)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF SARA CHEUNG'S MOTION TO QUASH DEFENDANT'S SUBPOENA TO THE CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS OF COMMUNITY PSYCHIATRY IS DENIED. ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE SUBPOENA DATED ON OR AFTER MAY 9, 2016 MUST BE PRODUCED TO DEFENDANTS WITHOUT ANY FIRST LOOK RIGHT BY MS. CHEUNG. ALL OTHER RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ARE TO BE PRODUCED ONLY TO MS. CHEUNG FOR A FIRST LOOK. WITHIN TEN DAYS OF RECEIPT OF THE FIRST LOOK DOCUMENTS, MS. CHEUNG MUST EITHER PRODUCE THE DOCUMENTS TO DEFENDANTS OR, IF SHE BELIEVES ANY OF THEM, SHOULD BE WITHHELD FROM DEFENDANTS, PROVIDE THEM TO THE COURT FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW WITH A COVER LETTER AND A COPY OF THE ORDER ON THIS MOTION. ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION THAT DEFENDANTS BELIEVE THEY NEED ABOUT THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES CAN BE OBTAINED THROUGH DISCOVERY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena To Plaintiffs Custodian Of Records, Community Psychiatry
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Meeti Sudame, Esq. 415-580-2575 of ILG Legal Office for Plaintiff SARA CHEUNG through Court Call.
Julie Stockton, Esq. 415-216-1203 of Littler Mendelson P.C. for Defendant BAL Migration.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Defendant does not contest the tentative ruling. Defendant seeks clarification on the Court's tentative ruling. The Court answers Counsel's questions. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SARA CHEUNG's Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena To Plaintiffs Custodian Of Records, Community Psychiatry.

Plaintiff Sara Cheung's motion to quash defendant's subpoena to the custodian of records of Community Psychiatry is denied. All documents responsive to the subpoena dated on or after May 9, 2016 must be produced to defendants without any first look right by Ms. Cheung. All other responsive documents are to be produced only to Ms. Cheung for a first look. Within ten days of receipt of the first look documents, Ms. Cheung must either produce the documents to defendants or, if she believes any of them, should be withheld from defendants, provide them to the court for in camera review with a cover letter and a copy of the order on this motion. Any additional information that defendants believe they need about the request for punitive damages can be obtained through discovery. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Off Calendar Oct-13-2017 Continued To Dec-13-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BARRY APPLEMAN & LEIDEN, LLP's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S UNVERIFIED 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Barry Appleman & Leiden, LLP's demurrer to the tenth through sixteenth causes of action in plaintiff Sara Cheung's first amended complaint is: a) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the portions of the tenth and eleventh causes of action alleging harassment and failure to prevent harassment and the fourteenth cause of action for IIED and b) overruled as to the portions of the tenth and eleventh causes of action alleging discrimination and failure to prevent discrimination, the twelfth cause of action for failure to accommodate, the thirteenth cause of action for failure to engage in the interactive process, the fifteenth cause for NIED and the sixteenth cause of action for negligence. Harassment and discrimination are two distinct claims (Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 646) and should not be combined together into a single cause of action. Ms. Cheung sufficiently alleges a discrimination claim. Paragraph 15 of the FAC expressly states that Ms. Cheung was "terminated due to her disabilities." The only allegation to support a harassment claim is paragraph 16 which alleges that "Ms. Reimer became increasingly hostile toward Plaintiff." Ms. Cheung has leave to plead ultimate facts as to what hostile conduct was severe or pervasive and altered the work environment. Paragraph 16 of the FAC adequately supports the failure to accommodate and failure to engage in the interactive process claims. Ms. Cheung alleges that defendant made it difficult for her to manage her disabilities, increased the notice time for appointments, and her supervisor was hostile when she requested an accommodation. Paragraph 19 also alleges that Ms. Cheung was immediately terminated after taking two days off for medical reasons. The FAC does not allege any extreme and outrageous conduct to support an IIED claim. At most the FAC describes personnel decisions. Such management decisions, even if improperly motivated, do not support an actionable IIED claim. The NIED and negligence claims are not barred by workers' compensation preemption because they are based in part on discrimination. (Fretland v. County of Humboldt (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1478, 1492.)

Order signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR No: 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)
